title: odontocarya tamoides var. paupera
intro: Odontocarya tamoides var. paupera, also known as the Paupera, is a plant species in the Menispermaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the Amazonian region, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Paupera are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health. In traditional medicine, Paupera is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion by boiling the roots and stems in water, and is also used to make herbal remedies such as teas and tinctures.
